description: Real-public-target A/B scenario. Target: news.ycombinator.com front page. Has TEXT-CONTENT jitter ("5 minutes ago", vote counts, comment counts, rotating front-page stories) which current DOM_NOISE_ATTR_PATTERNS (data-time/timestamp/frame/focused attributes) probably do NOT catch. This is intentional — AB3's job is to HONESTLY test the receipt's CURRENT pattern set against real prod page jitter shape.
rationale_target_choice: HN chosen because (a) real public site we don't operate, (b) no anti-bot wall — clean Camoufox loads, (c) predictable jitter shape: 'X hours ago' text + numeric vote counts + comment counts in visible text, NOT in data-time attrs, (d) no per-account state so cold sessions return identical-shape pages with only content jitter varying. Expected outcome: control < 100% (raw HTML varies), treatment likely ALSO < 100% (text-content jitter escapes current noise patterns). Evidence either way is useful — proves the pattern set's real-prod fit OR motivates per-site extension. WARNING — selector miss is a real failure mode here: an earlier draft used `table.itemlist`, which doesn't exist on HN (HN uses `table#hnmain` + `tr.athing`). A missing selector hashes the same empty region in BOTH arms → false 100% byte-stability → false huge positive delta. The companion `python/artifact_guard.py::detect_selector_miss_artifact` catches this. We re-run with `body` (broad-match, can't selector-miss).

control.description: Captures raw full_html — any jitter in the HN page surfaces here.

treatment.description: Broad-match region — receipt's dom_region_hash strips data-time/timestamp/frame/focused attrs IF present. The `body` choice intentionally avoids selector-miss artifacts (see rationale_target_choice).

expected_outcome.control_cache_friendly_score: Low — front-page stories change order/vote-counts every few minutes
expected_outcome.treatment_cache_friendly_score: Probably also low IF HN's jitter is in text-content not data-* attrs. Treatment ≈ control = honest signal that the default pattern set needs extending for real-prod text-content jitter. Treatment > control IF HN happens to have data-* jitter caught by the default patterns.
expected_outcome.wall_ms_overhead_bound: Mean delta should be <500ms in theory; in our published measurements it was +3017ms mean / +7.7s p95 on real Camoufox — real-prod overhead is significant, document it. N=20 gives enough samples for honest p95 + mean comparison.
